_readme: AUTO-GENERATED from .announcements during `bun run release:version`. Each item is one released version's in-app announcement. Do not edit released entries by hand.

items.text: Gemini (Gemini CLI) is now a fully supported provider — rich tool cards, plans, and reasoning, plus plan mode, steering, and slash commands. Sign in from Settings → Providers.
Workspaces now group by repository by default, and the agent-working indicator is a new animated Grex “G”.
Follow-ups now queue while the agent works — line up your next tasks above the composer and Steer, Edit, or Delete each one. Prefer interrupting the run instead? Switch to Steer in Settings.
The composer now shows an explicit Plan vs Auto mode, so it's clear when the agent is running with full access.
New chats can now inject selected previous sessions as context, so agents can continue work without pasting full transcripts.
🎨 We polished the interface across the composer, tabs, shortcuts, and empty states.
URLs in terminal output are now clickable — ⌘+click a link in the Terminal, Run, or Setup panels to open it in your browser.
⚡ Faster again — switching workspaces is now snappier and smoother.
Large pastes now stay as tags after sending — hover one to preview its full content.
Long messages now collapse to 20 lines — Show more expands them in place without losing your spot.
Quick panel: press ⇧⌥Space anywhere to spin up a task in a small floating window — pick a repo, send the prompt, and keep chatting without opening Grex. Hotkey configurable in Settings → Shortcuts.
New: Terminal Mode — run your prompt in the agent's own terminal UI (⌘⇧T).
You can now clean up all archived workspaces at once from Settings → General to free up disk space.
Claude Fable 5 (1M context) is now available at the top of the Claude Code model list — Anthropic's most capable model for the hardest, longest-running tasks. Opus 4.8 remains the default.
⚡ Major performance improvements across the app.
Cursor now supports Plan mode — it drafts a read-only plan first, then lets you approve it or request changes before it touches your code.
OpenCode is now a built-in coding agent — start a session and pick from any model it can reach, then connect your own providers in Settings.
Open Grex from your phone or any browser — pair a device once, then drive your desktop's workspaces and agents from a stable URL.
You can now build stacked PRs — a chain of small, dependent PRs. Plan one with /grex-cli stack, or split a change you've already written with /grex-cli break; the sidebar groups each stack together.
Agents now keep research notes and other scratch artifacts out of workspace Changes.
Gemma 4 12B is now available in the Local LLM installer.
On macOS, closing the window (or Cmd+Shift+W) now keeps Grex running in the Dock instead of quitting — click the Dock icon to bring it back. Cmd+Q still quits.
Use Command+Control+M or the header resize button to switch Grex between desktop and mini window sizes.
On narrow windows, the sidebars now peek in from the edges so the workspace stays full width.
Smart Triage now reasons more deeply before suggesting tasks, so its proposed tasks are more accurate.
Proposed tasks now show a source badge (GitHub, GitLab, Slack, or Lark) on their avatar, so you can see where each one came from at a glance.

Smart triage now surfaces only items that actually involve you (assigned, review-requested, @-mentioned, or your own work) and automatically archives proposed tasks once their pull request or issue is merged or closed — so the list stays relevant and cleans itself up.
Claude's dynamic workflows now appear as a live card, and the new /workflows command opens a keyboard-navigable drill-down into each run's phases, agents, and results.
Typing "workflow" or "ultrathink" in the composer now highlights the word to cue the matching mode.
Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) is now the default model, listed above 4.7 and 4.6 in the picker.
Queued messages now have an Edit button — click it to pull a queued message back into the composer for revision, leaving the rest of the queue in place.
Smart Triage: let the local LLM scan Slack / Lark / GitHub / GitLab and pre-fill AI workspaces for actionable items.

Coding agents in Grex can now drive Grex itself — spawn sibling workspaces, dispatch ship actions, and read other agents' transcripts. Just ask them in any chat. Grex CLI and skills install automatically in the background.
Every new workspace now has a gitignored `.agent-contexts/` directory agents can use to share files across sessions without polluting diffs.
Slack, GitHub, and GitLab context detail panels now refresh automatically when you open them or refocus Grex, and there's a manual refresh button in the toolbar.
Cmd+Shift+N now opens the new-workspace composer in Just-chat mode, and Cmd+N opens it in worktree mode.
Slack is now a Context source — connect your workspaces and the agent can read threads (with images) the same way you do.
Local LLM lands as a very experimental preview — it powers session title and branch name generation for now. More features coming soon.
Desktop notifications can now play a sound — pick from 12 effects in Settings → General.

Each run script can now declare an optional cleanup command that runs when you click Stop — handy for docker compose, supabase, or anything with external resources to tear down. A second Stop click force-kills if you don't want to wait.
You can now configure multiple run scripts per repository
Press ArrowUp / ArrowDown in the composer to step through previously-sent prompts for the current session — like bash or zsh.
Four new color themes are now available — Aubergine, Hoth, Choco Mint, and Banana.
Workspaces can now archive themselves automatically once their linked PR/MR is merged.
You can now edit code right from the diff view.
Send Grex feedback right from the app — file a GitHub issue or hit "Quick fix" to open a workspace with the feedback as the first prompt.
Pick "Just Chat" on the start page to open a workspace without binding it to a repo.
You can now reuse an existing branch when creating a workspace.
You can now hold Ctrl+Tab to cycle through recently used workspaces — release to switch, just like Cmd+Tab.
The sidebar now has view controls for filtering by repository, changing grouping, and choosing sort order.
The Git header now shows when PR checks are still running or GitHub is blocking a merge.
You can now choose a separate font for embedded terminals.
You can now turn inspector terminal hover expansion on or off in Settings.
Codex /goal continuations no longer ask for approval.
Appearance settings now let you customize chat size, UI and code fonts, and pointer cursors.
Codex slash commands now include skills from linked directories and the repository root.
Claude sessions now include the /goal command for setting a completion condition that Claude keeps working toward.
You can now drag workspaces in the sidebar to keep each section in your preferred order.
You can now group workspaces in the sidebar by repository — toggle it in Settings.
Add Context now supports GitLab too — auto-detected based on your current project.
Claude Code thinking now offers two modes. Choosing Omitted lets the final text stream sooner.
Plus a batch of performance fixes across the app.
